content.problemStatement:
There are a total of MMM possible types of marbles that you want to collect. They are numbered 1,2,‚Ä¶,M1, 2, \ldots, M1,2,‚Ä¶,M.

You currently have NNN marbles with you, with types A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. Note that you may have the same type of marble twice or more.

Find number of types of marbles that you still have not collected.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and MMM - the number of marbles you have, and the total possible types of marbles.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of types of marbles you have not collected yet.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You have not collected the type 333 marble only.
Test Case 2 : You have not collected types 1,2,‚Ä¶,41,43,‚Ä¶,1001, 2, \ldots, 41, 43, \ldots, 1001,2,‚Ä¶,41,43,‚Ä¶,100.
Test Case 3 : There is only one type and you have collected it (thrice).
